title: crystalliser operation
description: Crystalliser.  Two `model`s: `equilibrium` (default) is the cooling-yield model --- a solution is cooled to the operating temperature, the solubility falls and the excess crystallises, the mother liquor leaving saturated (yield, dissolved solute and cooling duty Q are RESULTS from the solute's `solubility {}` curve).  `MSMPR` is the continuous mixed-suspension mixed-product-removal crystalliser solved by the method of moments: the kinetics (referenced from constant/crystallisation) set the particle-size distribution, and the steady supersaturation, growth/nucleation rate, dominant crystal size and magma density are RESULTS.  One product: the magma (crystals in the solid phase + mother liquor).

operatingTemperature.title: Operating temperature
operatingTemperature.description: The temperature the crystalliser holds (an operating setpoint).  The mother liquor leaves saturated (equilibrium) or supersaturated (MSMPR) at this temperature; cooling it further raises the yield.  The cooling duty Q needed to reach it is a RESULT.

volume.title: Working volume (MSMPR)
volume.description: Working suspension volume of the continuous vessel (MSMPR model only).  Sets the residence time tau = V/Q, which together with the kinetics fixes the steady supersaturation and the particle-size distribution.  Ignored by the equilibrium model.
